commitMessage:
Add launch configuration name preferences (#526)

* Add launch configuration name preferences

- add preferences to use fully qualified names for launch configuration
  names (Java Applications, JUnit tests, and Java Applets)
- required for:
  https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.debug/issues/178
irrelevantReason: This commit introduces new classes, new preference constants, new UI elements, and new NLS entries. It does not modify existing code in a way that can be generalized into a reusable pattern-based DSL transformation rule. All changes are additions or declarations rather than transformations.
categoryReason: The commit's primary purpose is to add a new set of preferences and associated UI/logic, rather than transforming existing code patterns.
summary: This commit adds new launch configuration name preferences, involving new class files, preference constants, and NLS entries. It introduces new functionality rather than transforming existing code patterns, making it unsuitable for a DSL rule.

commitMessage:
Fix regression in debug tests caused by #535 (#541)

- fixes: https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.debug/issues/239
categoryReason: This is an internal bug fix in JDT's completion proposal logic.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires the ability to insert a new statement at a specific point in a block, which is not supported by current expression-level or simple statement transformation rules.
summary: This commit fixes a regression by adding a specific method call, `getDeclaringType(proposal);`, inside an `if` block. The DSL does not currently support arbitrary statement insertions into existing code blocks, making it a RED transformation.

commitMessage:
Create better fix for #572 (#545)

- fix IndentAction for text blocks to properly identify current
  line vs opening of text block
- enable IndentActionTest15 in JdtTextTestSuite
- add issue414_5 test
categoryReason: This is an internal bug fix in JDT's indentation action, involving structural changes to control flow.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires the ability to refactor control flow (e.g., breaking `else if` into separate `if`s) and insert complex nested `if` statements, which are not supported by the current DSL.
summary: This commit fixes an indentation bug for text blocks by restructuring control flow (breaking 'else if' into separate 'if' blocks) and inserting a new conditional block. These types of complex structural changes are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL, classifying it as RED.

commitMessage:
Add new formatter option for not separating html block tags in Javadoc (#544)

- required by:
  https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/issues/991
categoryReason: This commit introduces a new UI preference option for the JDT formatter, which involves adding new UI configuration logic. This is an enhancement of internal JDT UI functionality.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires the ability to replace a method call with one that includes a multi-statement lambda expression as an argument, involving the creation of new UI elements and their configuration within the lambda body. This level of structural generation and argument construction is not supported.
summary: This commit adds a new formatter preference option to prevent separating HTML block tags in Javadoc. It involves modifying a method call to pass a complex lambda expression that configures UI elements and their dependencies. This introduces new functionality and complex structural logic, making it unsuitable for a simple DSL rule and thus classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Avoid NPE in case there is no document provided by context (#556)

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/556
categoryReason: The change adds a null check to prevent NullPointerExceptions, aligning with null-safety practices.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL does not support inserting new if-statement blocks or control flow statements directly as part of a transformation rule. It primarily focuses on expression replacements or simple multiline additions that form a single conceptual expression or sequence.
summary: This commit adds a null check `if (document == null) { return false; }` to prevent a NullPointerException when the `fInvocationContext` does not provide a document. The DSL does not currently support the insertion of new `if` statements or arbitrary control flow constructs, only expression replacements or alterations of existing code structure within a match. Therefore, this transformation is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
[Mac/Win] Unable to add a new source folder to a Java project

... using the Java Build Path properties.

Fixed regression introduced via
https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/pull/243/ in
4.25.

The default wizard modality was changed from SWT.APPLICATION_MODAL to
SWT.PRIMARY_MODAL. This seem to break nested modal dialog opened from
the Java Build Path property page on Mac and Windows because the dialog
used wrong parent.

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/557
categoryReason: The change fixes a specific UI interaction bug related to modal dialogs and parent shells in Eclipse SWT/JFace, making 'Eclipse UI Fix' a fitting category for targeted UI behavior corrections.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL does not support inserting new statements (`action.setShell(getShell());`) into existing code at arbitrary positions. It primarily handles expression replacements, property modifications, or adding annotations/arguments to existing constructs. Inserting a new, independent statement is a limitation.
summary: This commit fixes a regression in Eclipse's Java Build Path properties by adding `action.setShell(getShell());` before `action.addPropertyChangeListener`. This ensures nested modal dialogs use the correct parent shell, resolving a UI issue on Mac and Windows. The DSL does not support injecting new, arbitrary statements before or after existing statements. This kind of structural insertion is a limitation of the current DSL, classifying the transformation as RED.

commitMessage:
Enhance NoStringCreationCleanUp (#552)

- modify cleanup so that it handles cases where the arguments
  to new String() are: nested new String() calls, method
  calls or variable references that are subsequently used to
  invoke String methods
- don't change new String() calls that are used to copy a
  String to another
- update preview content
- update CleanUpTest tests
- fixes: https://bugs.eclipse.org/bugs/show_bug.cgi?id=573119
categoryReason: Modifies the internal logic of an existing Java-based cleanup to fix bugs and enhance its behavior, rather than introducing a new, simple transformation pattern.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires more sophisticated AST traversal, contextual analysis (e.g., parent chain inspection), and flow analysis to conditionally apply or prevent transformations based on how the matched expression is used (e.g., if it's an initializer for a local variable, or part of a method chain that uses String methods). This level of conditional application logic is beyond the current DSL's expression-based matching and guards. Specifically, detecting nested `new String()` calls of arbitrary depth and making decisions based on the *outermost* `new String()`'s parent context is too complex.
summary: This commit enhances the existing `NoStringCreationCleanUp` to handle more complex scenarios involving nested `new String()` calls, and to conditionally apply the `new String(x) -> x` transformation based on the surrounding AST context (e.g., not replacing if it's a copy constructor for a local variable assignment). This involves sophisticated AST traversal and contextual analysis which is beyond the current DSL capabilities. It is a bug fix/enhancement to an existing Java-based cleanup.

commitMessage:
Add ability to specify used properties to avoid NLS search marking them (#562)

* Add ability to specify used properties to avoid NLS search marking them

- add enhancement to be able to specify XXXX.usedproperties file which
  contains property keys known to be used (e.g. a key that gets
  dynamically created where it is used such as in ResourceAction
- modify NLSSearchResultRequestor to read .usedproperties files if
  they exist and keep a set of used properties
- modify NLSSearchQuery to check if an unused key has actually
  been specified as used via the .usedproperties file
- add new test to NLSSearchTest
- for #555
categoryReason: Introduces a new feature to the NLS search, involving new fields, methods, and file I/O logic that are not expressible as pattern-based DSL rules.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires the ability to declare new fields and methods in the Java code block (`<? ?>`) and then reference them in rules. Also requires support for complex file I/O logic within the Java code block, which is not executable by the current DSL (only stubs). The rule refers to a method (`isSpecifiedAsUsed`) that does not exist in the source code.
summary: This commit enhances the NLS search to allow specifying properties that should be ignored, preventing them from being flagged as unused. This involves adding a new constant, a new internal `Properties` field, implementing logic for reading `.usedproperties` files, and modifying a boolean condition to incorporate the new check. The core functionality relies on new method definitions and complex file I/O that cannot be expressed in the current TriggerPattern DSL. Therefore, it is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Dispose javadoc hover widgets after delay (#528)

This change becomes effective with a product customization option set
like below (which would dispose hovers after 10 seconds if hidden):

`org.eclipse.ui/disposeClosedBrowserHoverTimeout=10000`

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/528
categoryReason: Relates to internal Eclipse UI behavior and lifecycle management.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL currently does not support inserting new statements after an existing statement (e.g., calling a method on a newly instantiated object on the next line). It primarily focuses on expression-level replacements or method-level annotation additions. Additionally, calling a private static method (`getDisposeHoverTimeout()`) from within a DSL rule would require explicit exposure of that method as a `@FixFunction` or a more advanced reflection mechanism, which is not currently available. Inserting arbitrary Java statements requires a more powerful AST manipulation primitive.
summary: This commit introduces a new private static helper method and calls it to set a dispose timeout on `BrowserInformationControl` instances immediately after their construction. The DSL cannot introduce new methods, nor can it insert a new statement (a method call) on a subsequent line directly after a constructor invocation. Chaining is not possible as `setDisposeTimeout` returns void. Therefore, this pattern requires advanced AST manipulation beyond the current DSL's capabilities, classifying it as RED.

commitMessage:
[21] Allow JDT to target Java 21 in compiler preferences

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/567
categoryReason: Modifies internal JDT UI code related to compiler compliance preferences.
summary: This commit updates internal JDT UI compiler preferences to allow targeting Java 21. The core pattern is updating a conditional check from `JavaCore.VERSION_20.equals(...)` to `JavaCore.VERSION_21.equals(...)`. This is a direct 1:1 constant replacement within a boolean expression, which is fully implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. Other changes involving array literal modifications and NLS message updates are not directly expressible with simple DSL rules.

commitMessage:
Add usedproperties files for known used messages in JDT UI (#564)

- part of #555
irrelevantReason: This commit adds new resource files (.usedproperties) which are not Java code and cannot be processed or transformed by the TriggerPattern DSL. It's an infrastructure/resource management change.
categoryReason: Not applicable.
summary: This commit is purely about adding new `.usedproperties` files, which contain key-value pairs and comments for NLS (National Language Support) entries. These are not Java code and therefore fall outside the scope of the TriggerPattern DSL for code transformations.

commitMessage:
Add ability to open the Call Hieararchy on Ctrl+Click (#575)

Add ability to open the Call Hieararchy viewer whenever the user clicks
on a method call or method or field declaration.
categoryReason: The commit introduces a completely new UI feature (Call Hierarchy hyperlink detector) rather than transforming existing code patterns or fixing a bug in such patterns. This is a structural addition of a class and NLS entries.
languageChangeNeeded:
The DSL currently focuses on code transformations (pattern-to-replacement). To support this, it would need capabilities for: 
1. Generating new classes and files.
2. Adding new NLS (National Language Support) entries to properties files and their corresponding keys to message bundles.
3. Automatically registering new UI components or contributions (e.g., via Eclipse extension points), which is outside the scope of code transformation.
summary: This commit adds a new UI feature, a hyperlink detector to open the Call Hierarchy. This involves creating new classes, adding NLS messages, and implementing new UI logic. This is a structural addition of functionality, not a transformation of existing code patterns, and therefore cannot be expressed as a TriggerPattern DSL rule.

commitMessage:
Add import removal support to VarCleanUp (#579)

- add new test to CleanUpTest10
- fixes #573
categoryReason: The change is an internal refactoring to an existing JDT cleanup mechanism.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs the capability to express programmatic actions on the AST rewrite engine, such as 'register a node for import removal' after a specific transformation (e.g., `ASTNodes.replaceButKeepComment`) has occurred. This is a meta-transformation that operates on the cleanup's internal logic flow rather than directly on the source code structure from `A` to `B`.
summary: This commit enhances the existing `VarCleanUp` by adding support for import removal. It involves adding a call to `remover.registerRemovedNode(node)` within the cleanup's `rewriteAST` method. This is a modification to the internal *logic* of an existing Java-based cleanup, instructing the rewrite engine to perform an additional step. It is not a pattern-based transformation of user-facing Java code and cannot be expressed in the current TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Fix regression in RenameLinkedMode

- add verification that iMethod root is same as root we are using
  NodeFinder on
categoryReason: The change is an internal bug fix in JDT's refactoring logic.
languageChangeNeeded:
The DSL currently primarily supports expression-level replacements and simple statement transformations. To implement this, it would need the capability to:
1. Insert arbitrary control flow statements (e.g., `if` blocks) around existing code blocks or statements.
2. Perform complex structural rewrites of multiple existing statements, which is a known limitation of the current DSL ('Complex multi-line statement blocks in replacements are NOT supported').
summary: This commit fixes a regression in `RenameLinkedMode` by adding an `if` condition to wrap existing code (`NodeFinder.perform` and `nodes.add(n)`). This is a structural change involving inserting control flow logic around existing statements. The current TriggerPattern DSL cannot express such complex multi-line statement block manipulations, making this a RED transformation.

commitMessage:
Add null check to CreateObjectReferenceProposal.findReference() (#599)

- fixes #597
categoryReason: Internal JDT fix for correctness in a quick-fix proposal. The fix involves adding new control flow.
languageChangeNeeded: Support for complex multi-line statement blocks and control flow (if/else, return) in replacements.
summary: This commit adds a null check within `CreateObjectReferenceProposal.findReference()` to prevent a NullPointerException. The change involves inserting an `if` statement with a `return null;` within an existing method, which is a structural modification of control flow that is not directly expressible with the current TriggerPattern DSL. Therefore, it is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Remove unused messages in JDT UI (#600)

- fixes #555
categoryReason: Removing unreferenced NLS message constants and their corresponding static String fields requires cross-file usage analysis.
languageChangeNeeded: DSL needs capability for cross-file reference analysis to detect unused elements (static fields, properties entries).
summary: This commit removes multiple unused NLS message keys from `JavaUIMessages.properties`, `RefactoringMessages.properties`, and `FatJarPackagerMessages.properties`, along with their corresponding static String fields in `JavaUIMessages.java`, `RefactoringMessages.java`, and `FatJarPackagerMessages.java`. Detecting unused NLS entries requires cross-file usage analysis, which is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL. Therefore, this is classified as YELLOW.

commitMessage:
Refactor NoStringCreationCleanUp to jdt.core.manipulation for jdt.ls use (#586)

* Refactor NoStringCreationCleanUp to jdt.core.manipulation for jdt.ls use

- fixes #577
categoryReason: Refactoring of an internal JDT cleanup implementation for architectural improvements (delegation pattern). This involves deep structural changes.
languageChangeNeeded: Support for changing class inheritance, deleting methods/fields, and more complex structural rewrites for class-level refactoring.
summary: This commit refactors the internal `NoStringCreationCleanUp` by changing its superclass to `AbstractCleanUpCoreWrapper` and delegating its logic to `NoStringCreationCleanUpCore`. This involves modifying class inheritance, changing constructor calls, and removing numerous methods. These are complex structural changes to a class's definition and implementation details that are not expressible with the current TriggerPattern DSL. Therefore, this is classified as RED. This commit refactors an existing Java-based cleanup plugin (`NoStringCreationCleanUp` is related to `sandbox_triggerpattern`).

commitMessage:
Fix MissingCodeTabPage to work with select all/deselect all (#585)

- register the annotations preference as well as the add SUID
  preference so that select all/deselect all works for them
- fixes #582
categoryReason: The commit fixes UI behavior related to Eclipse preferences within an existing JDT UI cleanup page.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need a mechanism to specify inserting new statements (method calls) at a particular point within an existing method body, possibly relative to other statements. This is a significant extension beyond current expression-level replacement capabilities.
summary: The commit adds two `registerPreference()` method calls to `MissingCodeTabPage.java` to ensure that 'select all/deselect all' functionality works correctly for 'Add Missing Annotations' and 'Add Missing Serial Version ID' preferences. This involves inserting new statements into existing method bodies, which is a structural change not supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL for pattern-based transformations. The DSL is designed for replacing existing expressions or declarations, not for inserting arbitrary new statements.

commitMessage:
Refactor VarCleanUp to jdt.core.manipulation for jdt.ls usage (#580)

- create new VarCleanUpCore based on VarCleanUp
- use wrapper of VarCleanUpCore to replace VarCleanUp contents
categoryReason: The commit involves a fundamental architectural refactoring of an existing cleanup class, including changes to its inheritance, internal implementation, and delegation pattern.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need extensive capabilities to: 1. Change the superclass of an existing class. 2. Replace entire method bodies with new logic (e.g., delegation). 3. Modify constructor calls to instantiate and pass a new wrapped object. This goes beyond expression/declaration replacement and would require class-level structural refactoring primitives.
summary: This commit refactors the `VarCleanUp` class by replacing its entire internal logic with a wrapper implementation that delegates to a new `VarCleanUpCore` class. This involves changing the class's inheritance from `AbstractMultiFix` to `AbstractMultiFixCoreWrapper<VarCleanUpCore>` and updating its constructors to instantiate `VarCleanUpCore`. This is a significant architectural refactoring, fundamentally altering the class structure and delegation pattern, which cannot be expressed using the current TriggerPattern DSL's pattern-matching and replacement capabilities.

commitMessage:
Fix NewClassWizard to prevent final super classes (#588)

* Have create class proposal flag a final super class as an error

- add new isSuperClassFinal() method to NewTypeWizardPage
- have NewClassWizardPage call doStatusUpdate() if super class is
  final
- Do not propose a super class that has final modifier
- add check to NewCUUsingWizardProposal.getPossibleSuperTypeBinding()
categoryReason: This is a fix to a UI wizard's behavior within the Eclipse JDT UI component, improving its correctness by preventing final superclasses.
summary: This commit fixes the 'New Class Wizard' to prevent proposing or using final superclasses. The primary pattern is the modification of a boolean condition in `NewCUUsingWizardProposal.getPossibleSuperTypeBinding()` to include a check for `!Modifier.isFinal(binding.getModifiers())`. This directly updates an existing expression to filter out final types. Other changes related to adding new methods or modifying method signatures are structural and not directly expressible in the DSL. The identified boolean expression modification is directly implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule.

commitMessage:
Move LambdaExpressionAndMethodRefCleanUp into jdt.core.manipulation (#595)

Signed-off-by: Jessica He <jhe@redhat.com>
categoryReason: This commit fundamentally reorganizes the project by moving an entire cleanup implementation between plugins, which is an architectural refactoring.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL does not support architectural refactorings such as moving entire classes or their implementations between projects/plugins. It is focused on in-code expression/statement transformations.
summary: This commit performs an architectural refactoring, moving the `LambdaExpressionAndMethodRefCleanUp` implementation from `org.eclipse.jdt.ui` to `org.eclipse.jdt.core.manipulation`. This involves deleting a large portion of the original class's logic and replacing it with a delegation to a new core cleanup class. Such large-scale structural and organizational changes are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL, which focuses on pattern-based code transformations. Therefore, this commit is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Use typed for-each loop (#617)

Avoiding type-casts makes code easier to read.

Co-authored-by: Jörg Kubitz <jkubitz-eclipse@gmx.de>
categoryReason: This change primarily improves code readability and type safety by refining loop variable types to avoid unnecessary casts, fitting into code style improvements.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs a mechanism to infer the most specific type for a for-each loop variable based on the generic type of the collection (e.g., `List<Expression>` implies `Expression` as loop type) or from common casts within the loop body. A new placeholder or guard function that returns the inferred type would be required for full automation.
summary: This commit modernizes enhanced for-loops by replacing `Object` loop variables with their specific types (e.g., `Expression`, `CPListElement`), thereby eliminating redundant casts inside the loop body. This enhances code readability and type safety. While a common and desirable pattern, the current TriggerPattern DSL lacks direct support for automatically inferring the optimal type for a for-each loop variable based on the collection's generic type or explicit casts of the variable within the loop body. Therefore, it is classified as YELLOW, requiring a minor DSL extension to fully automate this transformation.
dslValidationResult: Parse error at line 2: Expected '=>' or ';;' but found: $body$ (line 2)

commitMessage:
Codeminingfilter (#625)

* Add default filtering for code mining parameter names

- add filtering logic to CalleeJavaMethodParameterVisitor
- add new test to ParameterNameCodeMiningTest and fix old tests
- fixes #457
categoryReason: The change modifies internal JDT UI code to refine the logic for code mining parameter filtering, a common internal refactoring pattern.
summary: This commit refactors an internal JDT UI method call `skipParameterNamesCodeMinings(method)` by adding a `parameterNames` argument. This is a direct 1:1 method call replacement with an added argument, improving the internal filtering logic for code mining. This pattern is fully expressible in the TriggerPattern DSL and is a GREEN transformation, suitable for internal JDT modernization.

commitMessage:
Use short-circuit logic

To avoid calculation on right side.
categoryReason: The change improves the correctness and potential performance of boolean logic by using a short-circuit operator.
summary: This commit replaces the bitwise AND operator `&` with the logical AND operator `&&` in a boolean expression to enforce short-circuit evaluation. This is a common and safe modernization for boolean logic, improving correctness, readability, and performance. The transformation is a direct 1:1 operator replacement and is fully expressible as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule with type guards to ensure boolean operands.

commitMessage:
Remove extraneous if case in EnumConstructorTargetFinder (#634)

- fixes #622
categoryReason: The change removes redundant code, specifically a duplicated 'else if' block.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs the ability to perform structural analysis to identify and remove redundant control flow branches or semantically equivalent duplicate code blocks. This is beyond simple pattern matching and replacement of expressions or single statements.
summary: This commit removes a redundant `else if` block within an `EnumConstructorTargetFinder` method, addressing a duplicated code path. This is a structural code cleanup that requires semantic analysis to identify redundancy, which is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for direct pattern matching and replacement. Therefore, it is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Compare Array content instead of instance comparison

see
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/8777257/equals-vs-arrays-equals-in-java
categoryReason: Addresses a common correctness bug where Object.equals() is used for array content comparison instead of Arrays.equals().
summary: This commit addresses a common correctness bug where the default `Object.equals()` method was used to compare the content of object arrays, which performs reference equality instead of element-wise comparison. It systematically replaces `$array1.equals($array2)` with `java.util.Arrays.equals($array1, $array2)` (and its negated form `!equals()` with `!Arrays.equals()`) across multiple files. This is a crucial correctness fix. This transformation is already fully covered by two existing DSL rules: `arrays.correctness.array-object-equals-positive` for the positive case and `arrays.correctness.array-object-equals` for the negative case, both found in `probable-bugs.sandbox-hint`. These rules use `instanceof("java.lang.Object[]")` guards to ensure they apply correctly to array types. `Arrays.equals()` has been available since Java 1.5, so no specific `sourceVersionGE` guard is needed beyond the Java 8 baseline.

commitMessage:
remove unnecessary semicolon

reduce ecj warnings
categoryReason: Relates to code formatting and stylistic consistency.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs enhanced capabilities for matching and manipulating sequences of statements, specifically removing an EmptyStatement (` ; `) that directly follows another statement. Currently, patterns primarily target single expressions, declarations, or method calls/annotations, not arbitrary statement list modifications.
summary: This commit removes redundant semicolons (`;;`) after Java statements. Semantically, this involves removing an `EmptyStatement` node that follows another statement. The current TriggerPattern DSL is designed for transformations *within* expressions or single statements/declarations/annotations, not for arbitrary manipulation of statement sequences (e.g., deleting a specific statement from a list based on its predecessor). Therefore, this pattern is classified as RED because it requires DSL extensions for more sophisticated statement-level matching and rewriting capabilities.

Add new hover for Java hex, octal, and binary constants (#640)

- add new JavaConstantHover class
- add new hover to o.e.jdt.ui plugin.xml
- add new hover property strings
- fixes #639
- for Bug 559614
irrelevantReason: This commit introduces a new Java class to provide a new feature (a constant hover). It does not transform any existing code, which is the primary purpose of the TriggerPattern DSL.
summary: This commit adds a new Java class (`JavaConstantHover.java`) to implement a new feature (hover information for hex, octal, and binary constants). The TriggerPattern DSL is designed for transforming existing code, not for generating new classes or implementing new features from scratch. Therefore, this commit is not applicable for DSL rule mining.

commitMessage:
Add headless support split variable quick fix. Fixes #618 (#619)

* Add headless support split variable quick fix. Fixes #618
categoryReason: Refactors internal JDT API usage to simplify method calls.
summary: This commit primarily refactors JDT's `QuickAssistProcessor`. One part involves removing the `context` argument from calls to the internal `getAddStaticMemberFavoritesProposals` method. This is a direct 1:1 method call argument removal, which is fully supported by the TriggerPattern DSL and is a reusable pattern for internal API modernization. The rule is guarded by `inClass("org.eclipse.jdt.internal.ui.text.correction.QuickAssistProcessor")` to ensure precise application. A larger part of the commit replaces a complex method body with a delegation to a new Java-based cleanup (`SplitVariableFixCore`). While this major architectural change is not directly expressible as a DSL rule (RED), the identified argument removal is a clean, automatable, GREEN transformation.

commitMessage:
Merge method ref simplification support into LambdaCleanUpFixCore (#635)

- make the default behavior for a quick fix or Java feature convert

- add new preference under Java feature page for lambda conversion to
  simplify expression and make it true by default
- have the cleanup check for either the new preference or the

  syntax which fixes the issue that cleanups are not recursive so

- make quick fix default to use method refs
- add new tests to CleanupTests1d8
- fix cleanup ordering in jdt plugin.xml so when recursive cleanups
  are supported it will just work and duplicated code can be removed
- fixes #629
irrelevantReason: This commit introduces new UI strings (NLS entries) and adds new preference UI elements to configure an existing Java-based cleanup (LambdaCleanUpFixCore). It does not contain any code transformation patterns that can be generalized into DSL rules. The DSL cannot generate NLS properties or UI code.
summary: This commit adds configuration options for the `LambdaCleanUpFixCore` by introducing new NLS message keys and associated UI checkbox elements in `JavaFeatureTabPage.java`. These changes are related to UI and preference management, not to code transformation patterns. The TriggerPattern DSL is designed for code transformations, not for generating or modifying UI elements, NLS properties, or preference handling logic. Therefore, this commit is not applicable for DSL rule mining.

commitMessage:
Add headless support join variable quick fix. Fixes #641 (#642)

* Add headless support join variable quick fix. Fixes #641
irrelevantReason: This commit refactors the internal implementation of a quick-fix. It moves complex logic for identifying the 'join variable' pattern and performing the rewrite from `QuickAssistProcessor` to a new dedicated core fix class `JoinVariableFixCore`. The TriggerPattern DSL is designed to transform user code, not to refactor the internal Java code of Eclipse JDT UI plugins. The change from a large block of imperative code to a call to a new core class is an architectural change that cannot be expressed as a pattern-based DSL rule.
summary: This commit is an internal architectural refactoring of the JDT UI quick-fix mechanism. It moves the complex logic of the 'join variable' quick-fix into a new core fix class (`JoinVariableFixCore`). The DSL is intended for transforming user code, not for refactoring the internal implementation of Eclipse plugins. Therefore, this commit does not provide patterns generalizable into TriggerPattern DSL rules for user code transformations and is classified as NOT_APPLICABLE.

commitMessage:
Support cleanup quickfixes converting instanceof to isInstance lambda method reference

* Add support for isInstance lambda method reference

- add new logic to LambdaExpressionAndMethodFixCore to recognize
  a simple x -> x instanceof Y; pattern and to replace it with
  a method reference to Y.class::isInstance;
- add similar logic in LambdaExpressionsFixCore when converting
  from anonymous class method
- add similar logic to:
  QuickAssistProcessor.getConvertLambdaToMethodReferenceProposal()
- add new tests to CleanUpTest1d8 and AssistQuickFixTest1d8
- fixes #574
categoryReason: The pattern simplifies a verbose lambda expression into a more concise method reference, aligning with the goals of existing 'Lambda-Simplification' rules.
summary: This commit introduces a new quick-fix in JDT UI to convert lambda expressions of the form `x -> x instanceof Y` into method references `Y.class::isInstance`. This is a direct, semantically equivalent modernization pattern that improves code readability and conciseness, applicable for Java 8 and higher. The transformation is fully expressible as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule using lambda expression matching and type placeholders. An additional change in this commit involves using `ASTNodes.replaceButKeepComment()` which is an internal JDT refactoring already covered by an existing DSL rule (`jdt.internal.astnodes.replace.keepcomment`) and does not represent a new user-facing transformation pattern.

commitMessage:
Add refactoring proposal from if-return to ternary (#664)

- fixes #660
- add additional logic to AdvancedQuickAssistProcessor
- add new test
categoryReason: The transformation from `if-return` to ternary is a code style improvement, making the code more concise and readable.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need to support matching and transforming multi-statement patterns (e.g., an 'if' statement followed by a 'return' statement) into a single replacement statement (a 'return' statement containing a 'ConditionalExpression'). Currently, the DSL is primarily designed for expression-level or annotation-level replacements, not complex structural refactorings involving multiple statements.
summary: This commit adds a new refactoring proposal to JDT's `AdvancedQuickAssistProcessor` to convert `if (cond) { return thenValue; } return elseValue;` into `return cond ? thenValue : elseValue;`. While this is a highly desirable and reusable code style improvement, the transformation itself involves complex structural changes (matching multiple statements, removing one, and replacing another with a new statement structure). The current TriggerPattern DSL is designed for expression-level or single-statement replacements and does not support matching and rewriting such multi-statement control flow patterns. Therefore, implementing this transformation directly via DSL rules is not possible without significant DSL extensions. This quick assist is being implemented as imperative Java code.

commitMessage:
For lower-case for-loop variable without type, only suggest adding type (#658)

- fixes #657
- add new code in UnresolvedElementsSubProcessor to recognize when a
  lower-case enhanced for-loop variable that has no type causes a
  "create loop variable xxxx" proposal and then do not suggest
  creating new types, records, etc...
- modify existing test in UnresolvedTypesQuickFixTest
categoryReason: Modifies internal JDT UI quick fix generation logic related to AST structure and control flow.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL does not support changing method signatures (return type), adding control flow statements (if/return), or extracting/modifying local variables used for filtering proposals. It primarily focuses on expression-level and simple statement-level transformations.
summary: This commit modifies internal JDT UI quick-fix logic by changing a method's return type, adding return statements, and introducing new conditional logic to filter proposals for enhanced for-loop variables. These are structural changes to method signatures and control flow that are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
use some try-with resource (#672)

reduce ecj warnings
categoryReason: The commit introduces `try-with-resources` usage for resource management.
languageChangeNeeded: The current DSL primarily supports expression-level replacements and simple statement-level annotations/modifications. Converting a `try-finally` block to `try-with-resources` requires advanced structural transformation: identifying resource declarations, moving their initialization into the `try` header, deleting the `finally` block and its contents, and managing associated local variable declarations. This involves creating new nodes (TryStatement with resources) and deleting existing ones, which is beyond current DSL capabilities.
summary: This commit modernizes resource handling by converting multiple `try-finally` blocks into `try-with-resources` statements. This is a common and desirable Java 7+ modernization, but it involves significant structural rewrites of code blocks, including moving variable declarations, adding new `try` syntax, and removing `finally` blocks. Such complex structural transformations are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL, which focuses on expression-level changes. An existing Java-based plugin (`sandbox_try_with_resources`) already handles this, and the current DSL's `try-with-resources.sandbox-hint` only provides a hint, not a transformation. Therefore, this is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Jar loading: use try-with-resource

to close streams
categoryReason: This category already exists and covers resource management patterns.
languageChangeNeeded: The current DSL does not support structural transformations that convert a local variable declaration and subsequent code into a try-with-resources statement. It would require the ability to define a pattern that captures a variable declaration and a block of code, and then wrap that block in a new try-with-resources statement, which is beyond simple expression or statement replacement.
summary: This commit converts a manually-closed stream resource (`ZipInputStream`) into a `try-with-resources` statement to ensure proper resource management. While this is a highly desirable code improvement for resource safety, the transformation involves a structural rewrite that encapsulates a block of existing code (the `while` loop) within the new `try-with-resources` statement. The current TriggerPattern DSL is designed for expression-level and single-statement replacements/modifications and does not support introducing new control flow structures that wrap arbitrary code blocks. Therefore, this transformation is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
context-based-name-recommendation (#656)

* context-based-name-recommendation for extract temp variable
- for method invocation, look to see if expression has been assigned elsewhere to local variable and reuse if possible
categoryReason: The change directly modifies internal JDT API usage rather than user-facing code or general Java language features.
summary: This commit updates an internal JDT refactoring wizard (`ExtractTempWizard`) by replacing the `guessTempNames()` method call with `guessTempNamesWithContext()`. This is a direct, 1:1 method invocation replacement, which is fully supported by the TriggerPattern DSL. This transformation improves the context-based name recommendation logic for the 'Extract Local Variable' refactoring. It is an internal JDT API modernization and can be automated with a GREEN DSL rule.

commitMessage:
Don't add nls markers for lines covered by SuppressWarnings (#682)

- when doing a quick fix for missing nls markers and choosing to fix
  n instances of same warning, don't add missing NLS markers to lines
  covered by a SuppressWarnings(nls) whereby they don't cause a
  problem info of warning to be generated
- fixes #626
categoryReason: This commit modifies the internal logic of an existing JDT cleanup to fix a bug related to NLS marker generation, rather than introducing a new user-facing code transformation pattern.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL currently lacks capabilities to reason about and modify the internal implementation details (e.g., field declarations, assignments to internal fields, or complex conditional logic within method arguments) of existing Java-based cleanup plugins. It primarily focuses on matching and replacing code patterns in target source files. Implementing this would require language extensions for controlling internal state and conditional branching within rewrite logic.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in the `StringCleanUp` plugin (an existing JDT cleanup), preventing it from adding NLS markers to lines already covered by `SuppressWarnings`. This involves introducing an internal field (`fSavedCompilationUnit`) and modifying the `createCleanUp` method's argument with a ternary expression based on this field's state. This is a fix for an existing cleanup's internal behavior rather than a new generalizable code transformation pattern for user code, hence it is classified as RED. The current TriggerPattern DSL is not designed to modify the internal execution logic or state management of Java-based cleanups.

commitMessage:
Remove unused data from compilation unit annotation model util map #665 (#677)

The CompilationUnitDocumentProvider uses in its Java-specific annotation
model a utility map to navigate from document positions back to
annotations. The current implementation is slow whenever a document
contains plenty of annotations (such as the occurrences of the name of a
marked method), as the map internally uses a list for representing the
annotations which becomes slow in adding/removing when containing plenty
of data. This is especially a problem on batch replacements of
annotations (e.g. when selecting a different method to be marked in the
document), as every single annotation is processed by the map
individually. In addition, the map currently stores data that is never
used, as only the specific JavaMarkerAnnotations are used from that map.

This change thus does the following:
- Specializes the utility map to only contain the required type of
annotations
- Refactors the map implementation to simplify the code and make it more
comprehensible

Fixes #665
categoryReason: This commit refactors the internal data structure and logic of a core JDT UI component (`CompilationUnitDocumentProvider.ReverseMap`) for performance and specialization. It involves renaming a class, modifying multiple method signatures and their implementations, and specializing generic types, which are fundamental architectural changes not expressible by current DSL capabilities.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL currently lacks capabilities for complex structural rewrites, including: 1. Renaming an entire class (requiring updates to its declaration and all instantiation sites). 2. Changing internal field types from generic `Object` to parameterized `List<JavaMarkerAnnotation>`. 3. Rewriting method bodies that involve conditional logic, loop structures, and multiple statement changes (e.g., the extensive rewrites of `get`, `put`, `remove`, `addAnnotation`, `removeAnnotation` methods). 4. Automatically handling the cascade of internal method signature changes and the corresponding update of all call sites within a class. The current DSL is primarily designed for expression-level or simple statement-level pattern matching and replacement.
summary: This commit involves a major internal architectural refactoring of `CompilationUnitDocumentProvider.ReverseMap` (renamed to `ReverseJavaMarkerAnnotationsMap`) within JDT UI. The changes specialize its data structures (`Object` to `List<JavaMarkerAnnotation>`) and optimize performance by extensively rewriting the logic of multiple methods (`get`, `put`, `remove`, `setOverlay`, `addAnnotation`, `removeAnnotation`). These deep structural and logical changes are too complex for the current TriggerPattern DSL, which focuses on expression-level and simple statement-level transformations. Thus, this commit is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Move functionality and migrate callers from StubUtility2 to StubUtility2Core (#701)

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/700

Signed-off-by: Rob Stryker <stryker@redhat.com>
Co-authored-by: Rob Stryker <stryker@redhat.com>
categoryReason: This change involves internal JDT API modernization and restructuring.
summary: This commit refactors internal JDT code by moving the `createImplementationStub` method from `StubUtility2` to `StubUtility2Core` and updating all call sites. This is a direct 1:1 static method invocation replacement, which is fully supported and directly implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. The rule captures the class name change and the fixed number of arguments, ensuring that internal JDT API usage is consistent with the new architectural organization. The DSL automatically handles the corresponding import statement update. No specific Java version guard is needed as this is an internal refactoring not tied to new external language features beyond the Java 8 baseline.

commitMessage:
Add quick assist for deprecated method call (#704)

* Add support to wrapper into a fix proposal an InlineMethodRefactoring for deprecated method call that has Javadoc mentioning another method to use and the code actually uses that method and no references to private fields are made
- add new tests to AssistQuickFixTest1d8
categoryReason: This commit introduces new functionality (a quick assist) rather than a simple code transformation or modernization.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need to support complex semantic analysis, including binding resolution, Javadoc parsing (to extract @deprecated and @link tags), AST traversal (using an ASTVisitor to check method usage and private field references), and programmatic generation of complex refactorings (like InlineMethodFixCore). This is beyond the current capabilities of pattern-matching and direct replacement.
summary: This commit introduces a new quick assist to inline deprecated method calls that provide a clear replacement in their Javadoc. This functionality involves complex semantic analysis: resolving method bindings, parsing Javadoc tags, traversing the deprecated method's AST to verify the new method is used and no private fields are referenced, and then generating an `InlineMethodFixCore` proposal. This is a programmatic feature addition, not a simple pattern-to-replacement transformation. Therefore, it cannot be implemented with the current TriggerPattern DSL and is classified as RED. It extends the capabilities of the existing JDT UI quick-assist framework, which is a Java-based plugin.

commitMessage:
Used limit based viewers API #631

Adopt Package Explorer, Outline view contribution and editor breadcrumb
for Java editor to use new JFace API that allows viewers display huge
number of elements without freezing UI.

See https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/issues/818
and https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/pull/810.

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/631
categoryReason: The commit introduces new UI-related API usage for viewers.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires the ability to insert a new statement after an arbitrary statement (e.g., an assignment or another method call) or to define new private methods/blocks.
summary: This commit adopts a new JFace API (`WorkbenchViewerSetup.setupViewer`) for various viewers (JavaEditorBreadcrumb, JavaOutlinePage, PackageExplorerPart, JavaOutlineInformationControl) to improve UI responsiveness with large element counts. The calls to `setupViewer()` are inserted at different points in the initialization of various viewer types, making a single, generalizable statement insertion rule difficult with the current DSL, which primarily supports replacing existing expressions or statements. Additionally, the commit introduces a new private helper method `isExpandableNode` and new conditional logic using it (`if (isExpandableNode(element)) { return false; }`). The DSL does not support generating new methods or complex control flow insertions (like new `if` blocks) into existing method bodies, making this a RED transformation overall. If only the `setupViewer` part was considered as a hint to manually add the line, it would be YELLOW due to the varied insertion points requiring multiple specific rules or a DSL extension for 'insert after', but the `isExpandableNode` part makes it definitively RED.

commitMessage:
do not ignore whitespaces in string literals in java compare view (#584)

https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/560

Co-authored-by: Tobias Melcher <Tobias.Melcher@sap.com>
categoryReason: The commit fixes a UI-specific bug in the Java compare view.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires the ability to create new classes, add new fields, change method signatures, add new methods, and insert complex control flow statements (e.g., if blocks, variable declarations) into existing method bodies.
summary: This commit introduces a new `JavaIgnoreWhitespaceContributor` class and extensively modifies `JavaMergeViewer` and `JavaTokenComparator` to control whitespace ignoring in string literals and text blocks during Java file comparison. The changes involve creating a new class, adding multiple new fields and methods, modifying method signatures (e.g., adding a `boolean ignoreWhitespace` parameter to `JavaTokenComparator`'s constructor), and inserting complex control flow logic (e.g., new `if` blocks, local variable declarations) into existing methods. These structural transformations are far beyond the current TriggerPattern DSL's capabilities, which primarily supports expression replacements and simple annotation additions. Therefore, this is classified as a RED transformation.
